steps.title: Distribute to both terms
steps.content: To find an equivalent expression, multiply the outside factor $7x$ by each term inside the parentheses. This keeps the value the same for every value of $x$.

steps.title: Multiply each product
steps.content: Apply the distributive property: $$7x(x+4)=7x(x)+7x(4)$$ The first product is $7x^2$, and the second product is $28x$.

steps.title: Write the expanded form
steps.content: Combining the two products gives $$7x(x+4)=7x^2+28x$$ This expression is equivalent to the original factored expression. The answer is B.
